mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 labor market recovery from COVID-19 trough
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months with PAYEMS growth > 0.3% despite positive signal direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) begins accelerating, equity investors anticipate future monetary tightening. This triggers a negative forward return signal as markets reprice discount rates and growth expectations downward over the subsequent 60-day period.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 inflation expectations rising amid accommodative policy
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with policy lag and inflation emergence
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates consumer-driven demand, signaling robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion. This mechanism produces positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon as market participants rapidly incorporate demand strength into equity prices.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fiscal stimulus support
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum. Rising INDPRO combined with stable unemployment (UNRATE) signals productive capacity expansion without labor market stress, supporting equity valuations through improved earnings quality and reduced inflation risk.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 industrial production recovery and labor market normalization
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized supply and labor normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of periods where both INDPRO growth > 0.5% and UNRATE change < 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline signals weakening real estate demand and forward-looking economic pessimism. This mechanism predicts negative equity returns over 60 days as declining housing activity portends reduced consumer wealth, construction employment, and durable goods demand.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) decelerates, signaling labor market deterioration. This dual-signal mechanism predicts negative equity returns over 20 days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward and increase risk premiums.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 UNRATE at 5.1% with ongoing recovery
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or stalled recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real interest rates (proxied by FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL inflation trend) turn deeply negative, compressing equity risk premiums and supporting equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when real rates remain below -1.5% annualized.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 negative real rates amid low policy rates and rising inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 12-month rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of periods with deeply negative real rates.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) expand, signaling synchronized demand expansion. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 60-day equity returns as corporate earnings growth accelerates and economic momentum strengthens.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:April 2021 strong GDP growth expectations and retail sales recovery
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in post-shock recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.
